Off Calendar Dec-23-2020 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES:
James Parker of HUGO & PARKER (415-808-0300), appearing through CourtCall for Specially Appearing United States Fire Insurance Company, as insurer of Heieck Supply.
Geoff T. Sloniker of BRAYTON & PURCELL (415-898-1555), appearing through CourtCall for plaintiff and opposing party Robert Courtnier.

Matter heard simultaneously with Line 1. CGC-07-274229, Robert G. Baker vs. Asbestos Defendants

RULING:
Hearing held, argued and tentative ruling adopted: United States Fire Insurance Company's Motion to Set Aside Default and Default Judgment and for Leave to File an Answer in Intervention is granted in part and denied in part. The default judgment entered against Heieck Supply on March 30, 2018 is set aside only as related to United States Fire Insurance Company. The motion to set aside the entry of default is denied. United State Fire Insurance Company is granted leave to file an answer in intervention within ten (10) days of the entry of order on the instant motion. Order signed in open court. Judge: Cynthia Ming-mei Lee, Clerk: A. Mok. Reporter: Sheila Pham, CSR #13293 calendar-ca@veritext.com (Tel: 415-274-9977) (503/CML)

MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT Defendant Rheem Manufacturing Companys Notice Of Motion And Motion For Summary Judgment On Plaintiffs Complaint For Personal Injury
Off Calendar Nov-05-2015 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES:
Timothy D. F. Barteau of VASQUEZ ESTRADA & CONWAY LLP (415-453-0555), appearing for defendant / moving party Rheem Manufacturing Company
Kimberly J. Chu of BRAYTON & PURCELL LLP (415-898-1555), appearing for plaintiff / opposing party Robert Courtnier

RULING:
DEFENDANT RHEEM MANUFACTURING COMPANY'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT

Hearing held and tentative ruling adopted: Defendant Rheem Manufacturing Company's motion for summary judgment is granted. Defendant sustained its initial burden. Plaintiff failed to present admissible evidence to create a triable issue whether he was exposed to asbestos-containing products or materials attributable to defendant. Defendant's objections to plaintiff's declaration are sustained pursuant to D'Amico v. Board of Medical Examiners (1974) 11 Cal. 3d 1, 21. His declaration contradicted his deposition testimony. The Court did not get to Charles Ay's declaration. Order after hearing and order re: defendant's evidentiary objections signed in open court. Judge: Teri L. Jackson, Reporter: Rhonda R. Harris of AIKEN WELCH, CSR #7414 (Tel: 510-451-1580) (503/TLJ)
